findings.description: No rule was extracted for the PRIMARY color tier despite it containing a fully specified, mandatory palette (Brand Blue, Sunshine, Gold with HEX/RGB/CMYK/Pantone values). This entire cluster is missing from the candidate rules.

payload.rule_text: Primary palette (dominant tier) consists of Brand Blue #00529b, Sunshine #faa31b, and Gold #ffc60a, each with defined RGB/CMYK/Pantone equivalents.

findings.description: No rule was extracted for the SECONDARY color tier (Sky Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy) despite distinct tier heading and table values.

payload.rule_text: Secondary palette consists of Sky Blue #358CCB, Deep Blue #003D74, and Dark Navy #011E45 with RGB/CMYK values.

findings.description: No rule was extracted for the TERTIARY color tier (Coral, Light Blue, Golden Sand, Graphite, White) despite distinct tier heading and full table values.

payload.rule_text: Tertiary palette consists of Coral #F26A38, Light Blue #E6F0F9, Golden Sand #FEF1C8, Graphite #212121, and White #ffffff with RGB/CMYK values.

findings.description: Since no rules exist for this blob, there is no governance facet (hardness/polarity/scope/tier ranking) attached anywhere to the palette definitions, even though the source clearly signals a tiered hierarchy (dominant/secondary/tertiary) that should drive differing compliance strength (e.g., primary likely 'hard' mandate, tertiary likely 'soft' or accent-only guidance).

Missing relation: no cross-tier relation captured between Brand Blue (primary) and its tonal variants Sky Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy (secondary) and Coral (tertiary accent), which the palette structure implies (same blue family across tiers).
